content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description.txt:1-4 claims "AI-powered suggestions", "built-in ad blocker", and "works fully offline", but ContentView.swift only implements a plain local `tasks: [String]` list with no ML/AI framework imports, no ad-blocking/content-filter code, and no offline-sync or persistence logic found.

Pierre: Apple explicitly checks that marketing claims match actual functionality, and unsubstantiated "AI" or "ad blocker" claims are a common 2.1/2.3 rejection reason. The only code present is a trivial in-memory task list with no CoreML/CreateML usage, no NEFilter/content-blocker extension, and no persistence for offline claims. Either implement these features (e.g. add a real ML prediction model, a content-blocker extension, and local persistence) or rewrite the description to only describe what the app actually does.
